title: `COPY --exclude=subdir app/ app/` doesn't exclude `app/subdir`
body:
### Issue Description

When copying directory content, `buildah` expects `COPY --exclude` rule to include the dir itself.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

```
mkdir -p app/storage/cache
mkdir -p app/storage/views
```

```dockerfile
FROM ubuntu:24.04
COPY --exclude=storage/views app/ app/
RUN ls -la app/storage/
```

### Describe the results you received

Building this with `buildah` still adds `app/storage/views`.

```sh
$ buildah bud . 
STEP 1/3: FROM ubuntu:24.04
STEP 2/3: COPY --exclude=storage/views app/ app/
STEP 3/3: RUN ls -la app/storage
total 16
drwxr-xr-x. 4 root root 4096 Mar 12 17:52 .
drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root 4096 Mar 13 08:28 ..
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 4096 Mar 12 17:52 cache
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 4096 Mar 12 17:52 views
...
```

### Describe the results you expected

Building this with `docker` does not include `app/storage/views`.

```sh
$ docker build . --progress plain
...
#7 [2/3] COPY --exclude=storage/views app/ app/
#7 DONE 0.1s

#8 [3/3] RUN ls -la app/storage
#8 1.547 total 12
#8 1.547 drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root 4096 Mar 12 17:52 .
#8 1.547 drwxr-xr-x. 3 root root 4096 Mar 12 17:52 ..
#8 1.547 drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 4096 Mar 13 08:33 cache
#8 DONE 2.2s
...
```

### buildah version output

```yaml
Version:         1.43.0
Go Version:      go1.25.6 X:nodwarf5
Image Spec:      1.1.1
Runtime Spec:    1.2.1
CNI Spec:        1.1.0
libcni Version:  
image Version:   5.39.1
Git Commit:      
Built:           Mon Feb  9 13:49:51 2026
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

### buildah info output

```yaml
Fedora 43 x64
```

### Provide your storage.conf

```toml
--
```

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
<!-- Answer -->
This appears to be a Docker conformance issue with buildah's `--exclude` implementation. The `--exclude` flag was [added recently](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/5678) and has [known behavioral differences from Docker](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/6166).

The [pattern matching in buildah](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/79074e897647bf297cd7025cc05c16ca53f3184e/copier/copier.go#L1002-L1028) uses [containerignore(5) format](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/79074e897647bf297cd7025cc05c16ca53f3184e/docs/buildah-copy.1.md#L51-L54). As a workaround, you might try using a glob pattern:

```dockerfile
COPY --exclude=**/storage/views app/ app/
```

Or adding a trailing pattern to match contents:

```dockerfile
COPY --exclude=storage/views/** app/ app/
```

Alternatively, using a `.dockerignore` file might provide more consistent behavior:
```
app/storage/views
```

If none of these workarounds help, this would be worth tracking as a Docker conformance bug for the `--exclude` implementation.

The reference to the other issue is incorrect, but the workaround is good.
@Honny1 the workaround with glob is not specific enough. the second one is already bad.

> [@Honny1](https://github.com/Honny1) the workaround with glob is not specific enough. the second one is already bad.

I didn't test the second one, and I agree it lacks specificity. But looking at your reproducer, it looks good to me. Without clear specifications, neither a person nor an AI would know the difference.

Let me know if this fix handles your specific use case.
@Honny1 I switched to Docker to avoid losing more time on this, so I am not looking for workarounds.
